status: rq-06 resolved; rq-02 lane topology grounded

summary: code_graph_query performs readiness and query execution without per-query timers, percentile telemetry, or result-cache hit/miss accounting; the only cache-like behavior is the 5-second readiness debounce keyed by workspace/options.

Timing/cache telemetry exists today on adjacent surfaces: scan/parser code records parseDurationMs and scan durationMs, advisor briefs record cacheHit and durationMs, and advisor observability derives rollingCacheHitRate plus cache-hit rollingP95Ms; prompt-cache.ts itself exposes only TTL/eviction primitives.

The scorer is hard-locked to five lanes with weights explicit_author=0.45, lexical=0.30, graph_causal=0.15, derived_generated=0.10, semantic_shadow=0.00; semantic_shadow can score raw evidence but contributes zero live weighted score.

graph_causal is computed from explicit_author, lexical, and derived_generated matches rather than from an independent evidence stream, reducing effective live diversity to three independent primary evidence families plus one derivative lane.

A generic lane-ablation harness exists for all five lanes, but current parity assertions only require lexical ablation to reduce corpus accuracy; the scorer benchmark covers latency gates rather than lane-bias behavior.

RQ-06 is closed from source: code_graph_query has no query percentile telemetry and no result-cache hit ratio; existing timing/cache observability exists only on adjacent scan/parser and advisor-brief surfaces. Recommended remediation is split query timers, per-operation counters, and percentile histograms before any result-cache work.
